name: GoGogot
description: Lightweight self-hosted AI agent. Open-source OpenClaw alternative in Go.

commit.message:
Refactor Telegram channel interface and enhance message handling

- Updated the Telegram channel implementation to utilize a new `Replier` interface for improved message response handling.
- Refactored the `Channel` interface to replace `OwnerChannelID` with `OwnerSession`, streamlining session management.
- Adjusted the context management to support the new `Replier` structure, enhancing modularity and clarity in message processing.
- Revised the README to reflect changes in transport method implementations and clarify the extension process for new channels.

Refactor Telegram channel implementation and update dependencies

- Replaced the existing Telegram bot implementation with a new structure that utilizes a dedicated client for improved command handling and message processing.
- Introduced a new `client` package to encapsulate Telegram bot interactions, enhancing modularity and maintainability.
- Updated the `go.mod` file to include the `github.com/google/uuid` dependency and revised indirect dependencies for better compatibility.
- Removed deprecated code and streamlined the channel interface, improving overall code clarity and functionality.

Add Google UUID dependency and refactor channel structure

- Introduced the `github.com/google/uuid` package to the project for enhanced UUID generation capabilities.
- Updated the README to reflect changes in the model metadata path, now located under `internal/llm/catalog/`.
- Refactored the main application to streamline channel handling, including the transition from `Transport` to `Channel` interface for better abstraction.
- Removed deprecated code related to agent management and tool execution, improving overall code clarity and maintainability.

Update model management and documentation for enhanced clarity and support

- Modified the Makefile to change the output path for the OpenRouter model catalog, ensuring consistency with the new directory structure.
- Revised the README to clarify model selection requirements, emphasizing support for multiple providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter) and updating environment variable instructions.
- Enhanced error handling in the main application to validate provider and model combinations, improving user guidance and preventing misconfigurations.
- Removed deprecated OpenRouter catalog code and updated model resolution logic to streamline provider interactions.

Add Telegram bot commands for enhanced user interaction

- Implemented a set of commands for the Telegram bot, including "new", "chats", "memory", "stop", and "help".
- Updated the `Run` method to set these commands, improving user experience and functionality within the Telegram transport layer.

Enhance context management in scheduled task execution

- Updated the `RunScheduledTask` method in the Bridge to include transport context for improved task handling.
- Ensured that the context is properly managed with cancellation support, enhancing the robustness of scheduled tasks.

Revise README for clarity on model selection and use cases

- Streamlined the model selection section, emphasizing the requirement for both provider and model settings.
- Consolidated use cases to provide clearer examples of agent capabilities.
- Removed redundant explanations to enhance readability and focus on essential information.
- Updated the "How It Works" section to clarify the agent's operational loop.

Enhance model configuration and provider support in README and code

- Added support for the `GOGOGOT_PROVIDER` environment variable to specify the LLM provider (anthropic or openrouter) in the README and Docker configurations.
- Updated the main application to require the provider flag and validate model-provider combinations during execution.
- Enhanced model resolution logic to accommodate both provider types, ensuring proper error handling for unsupported models.
- Revised documentation to clarify the requirements for model and provider settings, improving user guidance.
Add logo to README for enhanced branding

Enhance build process and documentation for model management

- Updated the Makefile to include a new `generate` target that fetches the OpenRouter model catalog, improving model management.
- Revised the README to clarify model selection options, emphasizing built-in aliases and direct OpenRouter slugs for model configuration.
- Enhanced Dockerfile to automatically download the model catalog during the build process, ensuring up-to-date model information.
- Refactored the agent's model resolution logic to support both aliases and OpenRouter slugs, improving flexibility in model usage.
- Streamlined event handling and logging for model-related actions, enhancing observability and debugging capabilities.

Implement core agent functionality with event handling and tool execution

- Introduced a new `agent` package to encapsulate core agent logic, including configuration, session management, and event handling.
- Added support for tool execution with detailed logging and hooks for pre- and post-execution actions.
- Implemented compaction logic to manage message history and optimize context usage.
- Enhanced evaluation capabilities with an iterative evaluation loop for task execution.
- Integrated a comprehensive event system to track agent actions and tool interactions, improving observability.
- Refactored imports to align with the new package structure, ensuring better organization and clarity.

Refactor Telegram transport and skill management for improved functionality

- Replaced the `github.com/go-telegram-bot-api/telegram-bot-api/v5` dependency with `github.com/go-telegram/bot`, updating the transport layer to utilize the new API.
- Introduced a `storeAdapter` to facilitate chat and memory management within the Telegram transport.
- Refactored skill loading and management functions, moving them to the `store` package for better organization and clarity.
- Enhanced the `docker-compose.yml` to support dynamic image tagging based on the `IMAGE_TAG` environment variable.
- Updated the `go.mod` and `go.sum` files to reflect the new dependencies and their versions.

Add tag command to Makefile for versioning and Docker image tagging

- Introduced a new `tag` target in the Makefile to automate version bumping and tagging based on the current Git tags.
- Implemented checks to ensure a clean working directory before creating a new tag.
- Enhanced the Docker build process to include tagging with the new version alongside the latest tag.
- Updated the `.PHONY` section to include the new `tag` target for better build management.

Update README to enhance clarity and detail on agent features and deployment

- Revised the README to improve the description of the agent's capabilities and deployment instructions.
- Added a new "Quick Start" section with detailed Docker usage and local development instructions.
- Enhanced the features section to highlight built-in tools and model selection options.
- Removed outdated sections to streamline the document and improve user understanding.

Update Makefile, README, and Docker configurations for improved build and deployment

- Updated the Makefile to include new Docker build and push commands, enhancing the build process for multi-platform support.
- Revised the README to reflect changes in deployment instructions and clarify the use of environment variables.
- Modified the Docker Compose configuration to use the new image name and added the GOGOGOT_MODEL environment variable for better model management.
- Adjusted the Dockerfile to support multi-platform builds, improving compatibility across different architectures.

Enhance tool execution logging and status updates

- Introduced detailed tool execution logging by adding a new `extractToolDetail` function to capture relevant input details for various tools.
- Updated the `executeSingleTool` method to emit enhanced tool start events with detailed information.
- Refactored the `SendStatus` and `UpdateStatus` methods in the transport layer to accept structured `AgentStatus`, improving status message clarity.
- Enhanced the Telegram transport to format status messages with appropriate emojis and labels based on the tool's phase and detail.

Refactor chat storage and UUID generation for improved organization

- Introduced a new UUID generation method for chat IDs, enhancing uniqueness and consistency.
- Updated the chat storage structure to save chat files in date-specific directories, improving organization.
- Modified the chat loading and listing functions to accommodate the new directory structure, ensuring proper retrieval of chat data.
- Enhanced error handling for chat loading to provide clearer feedback when chats are not found.

Refactor logger initialization and streamline logging setup

- Simplified the logger initialization process by removing the file logging functionality, focusing on console output.
- Updated the `Init` function to accept only the log level, enhancing clarity and reducing complexity.
- Adjusted the main application to reflect the changes in logger initialization, ensuring proper logging setup without file dependencies.

Remove godotenv dependency and update README for environment variable handling

- Removed the `github.com/joho/godotenv` dependency from `go.mod` and `go.sum` as it is no longer needed.
- Updated the README to clarify that environment variables are now passed directly via `docker-compose.yml`, enhancing configuration instructions.
- Removed references to `.env` file usage in the setup instructions to reflect the new environment variable management approach.
- Deleted unused markdown files to clean up the codebase and improve maintainability.

Enhance scheduling functionality and command execution in the agent

- Updated the scheduling system to include an optional skill parameter for tasks, allowing for more complex multi-step procedures.
- Refined the command execution process to emphasize natural-language instructions rather than shell commands.
- Improved the prompt structure for scheduled tasks to clarify execution context and skill usage.
- Enhanced tool descriptions to reflect the new capabilities and provide clearer guidance for users.

Enhance README with detailed agent operation explanation and flowchart

- Added a new section explaining the agent's operational loop, emphasizing its simplicity and efficiency.
- Included a simplified version of the `Run` method to illustrate the core functionality.
- Added a flowchart to visually represent the interaction between user messages, LLM, and tool execution, enhancing understanding of the process.

Update dependencies and enhance README for improved clarity

- Added `github.com/yuin/goldmark v1.7.16` to `go.mod` for enhanced Markdown processing capabilities.
- Updated `go.sum` to include the new dependency and its checksum.
- Revised the README to improve formatting and clarity, including adjustments to use case examples and model information.
- Enhanced message formatting in the Telegram transport to support HTML, improving message presentation.

Refactor agent structure and update import paths for improved organization

- Updated import paths to reflect the new directory structure, enhancing clarity and maintainability.
- Removed obsolete agent files, consolidating functionalities into the core package.
- Refactored the main application to utilize the updated agent and tool management structure.
- Streamlined message processing and event handling within the agent for better performance and flexibility.
- Enhanced tool management by integrating relevant functionalities into the new structure.

Refactor agent and tool management structure for improved modularity

- Updated import paths to reflect the new infra structure, enhancing organization and clarity.
- Refactored the main application to utilize the new scheduler and bridge components for task execution.
- Consolidated tool management by removing obsolete tool files and integrating relevant functionalities into the infra package.
- Enhanced message processing in the agent by utilizing a unified content block structure, improving flexibility and maintainability.
- Streamlined event handling in the Bridge agent to support new task execution models and improve overall performance.

Refactor agent event handling and enhance message processing

- Updated the agent to utilize a unified event structure, replacing the previous event handling system.
- Refactored the Run method to accept a slice of content blocks, improving flexibility in message processing.
- Removed obsolete event and hook files to streamline the codebase.
- Enhanced the Bridge agent to support new event handling and message processing for various media types.
- Improved attachment processing in the Telegram transport to handle additional media formats, including audio and video.

Implement local tool management and enhance prompt handling in the agent

- Introduced local tool definitions and execution methods to support session-scoped tools.
- Updated the agent's Run method to utilize local tools, improving task execution flexibility.
- Enhanced the summary prompt in compaction configuration to include task plan checklist state.
- Added a new work strategy section to the system prompt for better task management guidance.
- Updated LLM client call options to accommodate additional tools, enhancing integration capabilities.

Refactor agent architecture to remove orchestration module and streamline event handling

- Replaced orchestration references with direct agent implementations, enhancing modularity and clarity.
- Updated event handling to utilize a unified event structure, improving consistency across the agent's operations.
- Consolidated compaction and evaluation logic into the agent, simplifying the overall architecture.
- Removed obsolete orchestration files to clean up the codebase and reduce complexity.
- Enhanced prompt handling by integrating prompt context directly into the agent configuration.

Enhance timezone handling and update agent configuration

- Implemented timezone loading from user.md, allowing for localized scheduling and task execution.
- Refactored agent configuration to use a context struct for prompts, improving modularity.
- Updated README to clarify timezone requirements and user.md structure for better user guidance.
- Enhanced scheduler to adapt to timezone changes dynamically, ensuring accurate task execution timing.

Update README to enhance clarity and include session cost details for LLM models

- Revised the model pricing table to incorporate session cost estimates alongside input and output pricing.
- Added a note clarifying the typical session token usage for better understanding of costs.

Refactor logging, update LLM provider integration, and enhance README

- Switch logging to Zerolog and update dependencies
- Refactor LLM provider integration with unified types package
- Improve README clarity and detail on project features

Made-with: Cursor

Refactor LLM integration to use unified types package

- Replaced all instances of the anthropic package with a new types package for message and content block handling across the agent and orchestration modules.
- Updated related functions and structures to ensure compatibility with the new types.
- Removed the obsolete anthropic types file to streamline the codebase.

Enhance scheduler functionality and task execution in main application

- Refactored the scheduler to run tasks in-process, allowing for direct command execution and result delivery to the owner.
- Implemented exponential backoff for task retries on failure, improving error handling.
- Updated task state management to track execution status, errors, and durations.
- Modified the main application to integrate the new task execution model and added a dedicated executor for scheduled tasks.
- Improved documentation for scheduling tools to reflect the new execution model and task state information.

Refactor event handling in Bridge agent to use a dedicated events channel

- Introduced a new events channel in the Bridge agent for better event management
- Updated consumeEvents function to accept the new events channel instead of the agent's internal events
- Ensured proper closure of the events channel after agent execution

Refactor agent structure, enhance README, and improve Telegram transport

- Update README.md: add badges, improve clarity, remove obsolete tool reference section
- Refactor SystemPrompt to accept a context struct for modularity
- Update agent configuration with default compaction settings
- Remove obsolete agent specification and related files
- Improve build command handling in Telegram transport (new chats, error handling)
- Enhance core identity message with undefined identity note

Made-with: Cursor

Initial commit: Sofie AI agent with orchestration, scheduling, and tools

Made-with: Cursor

releases.body:
# v0.0.10

## Native OpenAI Provider

OpenAI is now a first-class provider. Set `GOGOGOT_PROVIDER=openai` with `OPENAI_API_KEY` to use 14 models directly (GPT-5.x, GPT-4.1, GPT-4o, o3/o4 family) — no OpenRouter proxy needed.

## Unified Model Catalog

Model metadata for all providers moved to editable JSON files in `llm/catalog/`. Anthropic catalog expanded from 1 to 7 models (added Opus 4.6/4.5, Sonnet 4.5/4, Haiku 4.5/3.5). To add a model — just edit JSON.

## Telegram Bot Commands

Bot now registers `/new`, `/chats`, `/memory`, `/stop`, `/help` in the Telegram menu.

## Startup Error Handling

Invalid config (wrong provider, missing API key) now sends an error to the owner via Telegram instead of crash-looping.

## Breaking Changes

- New provider value: `openai` (alongside `anthropic`, `openrouter`)
- Alias `gemini` now resolves to `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` (was `-pro-`)
- New alias: `openai` → `openai/gpt-5-nano`

releases.name: v0.0.9 - fix sheduler transport
releases.body:
## v0.0.9

### Bug Fixes

- **Fix: scheduled tasks now have transport context** — Scheduled (cron) tasks previously ran without access to the transport layer, which meant they couldn't send messages back to the user (e.g., via Telegram). The transport context is now properly injected into `RunScheduledTask`, so scheduled tasks can communicate results as expected. (`core/bridge/bridge.go`)

releases.name: v0.0.8
releases.body:
## v0.0.8

### Breaking: Explicit Provider Selection

`GOGOGOT_PROVIDER` (`anthropic` | `openrouter`) and `GOGOGOT_MODEL` are now **required**. The agent no longer auto-detects the provider from available API keys.

```bash
# Add to your .env / environment:
GOGOGOT_PROVIDER=openrouter   # new, required
GOGOGOT_MODEL=deepseek        # was optional, now required
```

### Changes

- **`GOGOGOT_PROVIDER` env var + `--provider` CLI flag** — explicit provider selection, replaces auto-detection
- **Cross-provider alias routing** — `claude` alias resolves correctly for both Anthropic and OpenRouter
- **Clearer error messages** on misconfigured provider/model
- **README** — added logo, model alias table, updated Docker examples
- **Docker Compose** — added `GOGOGOT_PROVIDER` as required variable

releases.name: v0.0.7
releases.body:
## v0.0.7

### Highlights

- **Modular agent core** — split into `core/agent/event`, `core/agent/hook`, `core/agent/session` with backward-compatible re-exports.
- **Structured event system** — typed events for LLM calls, tool executions, compaction, eval runs.
- **Before/after LLM hooks** — observe and log every LLM request with full context.
- **OpenRouter catalog** — model metadata (context, vision, pricing) auto-populated at build time via `make generate`. No more manual `models.json`.
- **Any model via slug** — pass any OpenRouter slug directly: `GOGOGOT_MODEL=openai/gpt-5.4`.
- **Telegram transport refactor** — cleaner handlers, extracted constants, simplified media & skill management.

### Breaking

- Removed `models.json`, `llm/debug.go`, `llm/pricing.go` — custom models now via OpenRouter slug.
- Packages moved under `core/` — update import paths.

releases.name: v0.0.6
releases.body:
### Features
- Skill-aware scheduler — scheduled tasks support multi-step procedures via optional `skill` parameter
- Rich tool status in Telegram with phase indicators and descriptive labels
- Detailed tool execution logging with input context

### Improvements
- Multi-platform Docker builds (`docker buildx`), new `make tag` for automated versioning
- Chat storage moved to UUID-based IDs in date-organized directories
- Simplified logger — console-only, removed file logging
- Removed `godotenv` — env vars via `docker-compose.yml` directly
- README rewritten with clearer quick-start and deployment docs

### Removed
- `markdown/` package (~800 lines) — formatting consolidated into `transport/telegram/format.go`

readme_html:
GoGogot — Lightweight OpenClaw Written in Go

A lightweight, extensible, and secure open-source AI agent that lives on your server. It runs shell commands, edits files, browses the web, manages persistent memory, and schedules tasks — a self-hosted alternative to OpenClaw (Claude Code) in ~4,500 lines of Go.

  • Single binary, ~15 MB, ~10 MB RAM — deploys with one docker run command
  • Your keys stay on your server — no cloud account, no telemetry, no phoning home
  • You pick the model — Anthropic, OpenAI, or any OpenRouter model
  • Extensible — clean Go interfaces (Backend, Transport, Tool) make it trivial to add providers, transports, or custom tools

Quick Start

Prerequisites

  • Get a TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN by creating a new bot via @BotFather on Telegram.
  • Find your TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID (your personal Telegram user ID) using a bot like @userinfobot. This is critical for security — it ensures only you can communicate with your agent.

Docker

No git clone needed — the image is published on Docker Hub:

docker run -d --restart unless-stopped \
  --name gogogot \
  -e TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=... \
  -e TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID=... \
  -e GOGOGOT_PROVIDER=anthropic \
  -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... \
  -e GOGOGOT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6 \
  -v ./data:/data \
  -v ./work:/work \
  octagonlab/gogogot:latest

The image supports linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 and ships with a full Ubuntu environment (bash, git, Python, Node.js, ripgrep, sqlite, postgresql-client, and more).

curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aspasskiy/GoGogot/main/deploy/docker-compose.yml

# Create .env with your keys
cat > .env <<EOF
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=...
TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID=...
GOGOGOT_PROVIDER=anthropic
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=...
GOGOGOT_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6
EOF

docker compose up -d

Requires Go 1.25+:

make generate          # fetch OpenRouter model catalog
go run ./cmd

Choosing a Model

Set GOGOGOT_PROVIDER, GOGOGOT_MODEL, and the corresponding API key. The agent will not start without all three.

Provider GOGOGOT_PROVIDER API key env Example GOGOGOT_MODEL
Anthropic anthropic ANTHROPIC_API_KEY claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5
OpenAI openai OPENAI_API_KEY gpt-4o, gpt-4.1, gpt-5.4, o3, o4-mini
OpenRouter openrouter OPENROUTER_API_KEY deepseek/deepseek-v3.2, google/gemini-3-flash-preview

Model metadata (context window, vision support, pricing) is stored in JSON catalogs under llm/catalog/ — just edit the JSON to add or update models.

With OpenRouter you can also pass any slug directly, e.g. GOGOGOT_MODEL=moonshotai/kimi-k2.5.

Short Aliases

For convenience, short aliases are supported as GOGOGOT_MODEL values:

Alias Resolves to
claude claude-sonnet-4-6
openai openai/gpt-5-nano
deepseek deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
gemini google/gemini-3-pro-preview
llama meta-llama/llama-4-maverick
qwen qwen/qwen3.5-397b-a17b
minimax minimax/minimax-m2.5
kimi moonshotai/kimi-k2.5

Browse all available models: Anthropic | OpenAI | OpenRouter | Benchmarks: PinchBench

Features

27 built-in tools across 10 categories:

  • Telegram — multi-chat, attachments, typing indicators
  • System — bash, read/write/edit files, system info
  • Web — Brave search, fetch pages, HTTP requests, downloads
  • Identity — persistent soul.md / user.md, auto-evolving
  • Memory — persistent markdown notes the agent manages itself
  • Skills — reusable procedural knowledge
  • Task planning — session-scoped checklist for multi-step work
  • Scheduling — cron-based self-scheduling, persisted across restarts
  • Compaction — automatic context compression near token limits
  • Multi-model — Anthropic, OpenAI, or any OpenRouter model
  • Observability — structured events (LLM calls, tool runs, errors)

Use Cases

  • Daily digest — "Find top 5 AI news, summarize each in 2 sentences, send me every morning at 9:00"
  • Report generation — "Download sales data from this URL, calculate totals by region, generate a PDF report"
  • File processing — "Take these 12 screenshots, merge them into a single PDF, and send the file back"
  • Market research — "Search the web for pricing of competitors X, Y, Z and make a comparison table"
  • Server monitoring — "Check disk and memory usage every hour, alert me if anything exceeds 80%"
  • Data extraction — "Fetch this webpage, extract all email addresses and phone numbers into a CSV"
  • Routine automation — "Every Friday at 18:00, pull this week's git commits and send me a changelog summary"

How It Works

The entire agent is a for loop. Call the LLM, execute tool calls, feed results back, repeat:

func (a *Agent) Run(ctx context.Context, input []ContentBlock) error {
    a.messages = append(a.messages, userMessage(input))

    for {
        resp, err := a.llm.Call(ctx, a.messages, a.tools)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        a.messages = append(a.messages, resp)

        if len(resp.ToolCalls) == 0 {
            break
        }

        results := a.executeTools(resp.ToolCalls)
        a.messages = append(a.messages, results)
    }
    return nil
}

Everything else — memory, scheduling, compaction, identity — is just tools the LLM can call inside this loop.

Extending

GoGogot is designed to be extended without frameworks or plugin registries:

  • Adding a new LLM backend (implement one Backend interface method)
  • Adding a new transport like Discord or Slack (implement Channel + Replier — 3 + 6 methods)
  • Adding custom models by editing JSON catalogs in llm/catalog/

License

MIT
